skill.name: doc-coauthoring
skill.description: Guides users through context gathering, iterative drafting, and reader testing for documentation, proposals, technical specs, and decision documents.
skill.summary: Co-author structured documents through guided context gathering, drafting, refinement, and reader testing.
skill.author: 7Spade

security_audit.summary: The static shell execution and weak cryptography findings are false positives caused by markdown backticks and normal documentation prose. The skill still has moderate operational risk because it can create or edit document files and encourages user-authorized connector access to shared documents or team channels.

medium_findings.title: User-Authorized Connector Access to Shared Content
medium_findings.description: The workflow asks users to provide links, team channels, or shared documents and then uses available integrations to read that content. This is legitimate for documentation co-authoring, but it can expose sensitive organizational context if users grant broad connector access without checking scope.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file explicitly instructs connector-based reading of shared documents and team channels. The same section frames this as user-directed access, so the risk is moderate rather than malicious.

medium_findings.title: Document File Creation and Replacement Workflow
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs the assistant to create markdown files or artifacts and to replace document sections during drafting. This is expected behavior for a co-authoring skill, but users should verify file targets and review edits before accepting them.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines directly instruct file or artifact creation and text replacement. There is no shell execution, but filesystem modification is an operational risk.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Tool Names Flagged as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The static analyzer identified Ruby or shell backtick execution, but the cited locations are markdown-formatted tool names or prose instructions. No evidence found of shell command construction, process execution, or user-controlled command injection.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited strings are markdown backticks around assistant tool names and filenames. There is no executable code or command interpreter syntax at those locations.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern Did Not Match Cryptography
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged weak cryptographic algorithms at prose lines, but those lines describe the documentation workflow, audience questions, and user feedback. No evidence found of MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or cryptographic API usage.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain ordinary markdown content and workflow instructions. Manual review found no cryptographic code or weak algorithm reference.

low_findings.title: Benign Hardcoded Claude URL for Manual Testing
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URL points users to Claude for manual reader testing. This is a normal product link and does not transmit data by itself, but users should avoid pasting sensitive documents unless allowed by their policy.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL is a public Claude application link used in manual instructions. There is no hidden network request, webhook, or exfiltration endpoint.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Network Reconnaissance Pattern in Documentation Text
low_findings.description: The network reconnaissance alert maps to workflow text about gathering and tracking document context. No evidence found of scanning hosts, ports, services, or networks.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line is an option for a user information dump. It does not contain reconnaissance tooling, network targets, or scanning instructions.

content.value_statement: Writing useful documentation often fails when context stays in the author's head. This skill guides Claude, Codex, or Claude Code through gathering context, drafting sections, refining text, and testing reader understanding.
actual_capabilities: Guides a three-stage workflow for context gathering, drafting, and reader testing.
Asks clarifying questions before drafting substantial documentation.
Creates a document scaffold with section headings and placeholders.
Brainstorms, curates, drafts, and refines one section at a time.
Uses fresh-reader testing to find unclear assumptions and contradictions.
Supports artifacts, local markdown files, and manual shared-document workflows.
limitations: It does not verify factual accuracy beyond the context and sources provided.
Connector access depends on the user's environment and permissions.
It can create or edit draft files, so users should review changes.
Reader testing is simulated or manual and may not represent every real audience.

use_cases.title: Draft a Technical Spec
use_cases.description: Turn scattered engineering context into a structured technical proposal with clear decisions, trade-offs, and open questions.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Decision Document
use_cases.description: Capture background, alternatives, rationale, and expected impact before sharing a decision with stakeholders.

use_cases.title: Improve Existing Documentation
use_cases.description: Review an existing document for missing context, unclear assumptions, contradictions, and sections that need tighter wording.

prompt_templates.title: Start a New Doc
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner workflow for a new document.
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me co-author a new document. Ask me the setup questions first, then guide me through context gathering before drafting.

prompt_templates.title: Structure a Proposal
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate workflow for a proposal without a clear outline.
prompt_templates.prompt: I need to write a proposal but do not know the best structure. Interview me, suggest sections, and create a scaffold.

prompt_templates.title: Refine Section by Section
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced workflow for improving a rough draft.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the co-authoring workflow on this draft. Work one section at a time, ask focused questions, and make precise edits.

prompt_templates.title: Run Reader Testing
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced workflow for validating a nearly complete document.
prompt_templates.prompt: Test this document as a fresh reader. Predict likely reader questions, identify ambiguous assumptions, and recommend targeted fixes.

output_examples.input: I need a decision doc about replacing our deployment process.
output: A short set of setup questions about audience, desired impact, constraints, and template needs.
A proposed section outline covering context, options, recommendation, risks, rollout, and open questions.
A staged plan for drafting the most uncertain section first.

output_examples.input: Here is a rough technical spec. Make it work for reviewers.
output: A list of unclear assumptions and missing reader context.
Focused questions for the technical approach section.
Precise edits that improve clarity without rewriting the whole document.

output_examples.input: We are almost done. Check if readers will understand this.
output: Likely reader questions based on the document title and content.
A fresh-reader assessment of ambiguous wording, missing context, and contradictions.
A short fix list ordered by reader impact.

best_practices: Provide audience, goal, constraints, and source context before asking for a draft.
Work section by section so the assistant can learn your style and priorities.
Review file edits and connector-sourced content before sharing the document.
anti_patterns: Do not ask for a complete polished document before providing enough context.
Do not grant connector access to private channels unless the content is approved for use.
Do not skip reader testing when the document must influence decisions.

faq.question: What document types does this skill support?
faq.answer: It supports technical specs, proposals, decision documents, RFCs, PRDs, and similar structured writing.

faq.question: Can it edit an existing document?
faq.answer: Yes. It can review existing content and suggest or apply focused edits when the environment supports file editing.

faq.question: Does it need connector access?
faq.answer: No. Connector access is optional. Users can paste context directly when connectors are unavailable or inappropriate.

faq.question: How does reader testing work?
faq.answer: The skill tests whether a fresh reader can answer likely questions from the document without hidden context.

faq.question: Is it safe for confidential documents?
faq.answer: Use it only with approved tools and approved data. Review connector permissions and avoid sharing restricted information.

faq.question: Will it guarantee factual accuracy?
faq.answer: No. It improves structure, clarity, and reader fit, but users must verify facts, links, and technical details.
